hints.text: Plate: long **white** (incl. back) + blue strip left — **white backplate** distinguishes from UK (yellow back)
**Yellow dashed outer road lines** — common in Ireland, ~never in UK (also NZ)
**Only European country with yellow diamond warning signs** (regulatory signs stay red/white)
Pole sticker: lightning bolt but **no man being zapped** (UK sticker has the man)
Green+white bollards; rural gates **rounded corners** (UK square; but N. Ireland also rounded)
"Boreens" — tiny rural roads with a **strip of grass down the middle**; drives on the left; smallcam
Cream-white buildings w/ black roofs, sometimes grey corner stones
Regional roads = 3-digit; **first digit = region**, numbered **anti-clockwise from Dublin**
Phone area codes grouped by first digit (learnable) → region
West coast = rugged hilly moorland, brownish/yellow grass, exposed rock (vs neat green fields of the east)
**EV Gen4 car** (grey back w/ black semicircle) covers most of the east & parts of NW
Distinctive flat **tabletop mountains** → north Sligo / Leitrim; hilly tiny Gen4 roads → Donegal
